summary: Extended manifest-based detection in Language::from_directory to cover all 18 supported languages. Added 15 new ManifestKind variants (7 langs × ~2 families each) using a new ManifestMatcher enum that supports fixed filenames, file-extension globs (*.csproj, *.opam, *.rockspec), and nested fixed paths (project/build.properties). Resolved .h ambiguity between C and C++ via a source-file tie-break that counts cpp-family extensions (.cpp/.cc/.cxx/.hpp/.hh/.hxx) vs the strictly-C .c extension. Added 38 unit tests + 18 integration tests + 1 parser health audit. All 18 languages now detect correctly under bait conditions; all 18 parsers parse their minimal valid snippet with zero ERROR nodes.

parser_audit.method: ParserPool::parse on a minimal valid snippet for each language, counting ERROR nodes in the resulting tree.
parser_audit.result: all 18 parsers return 0 ERROR nodes on a minimal snippet

snippets_used.Java: class X { public static void main(String[] a){} }
snippets_used.Scala: object X { def main(args: Array[String]): Unit = {} }

per_language_status.manifest_coverage: VAL-008 NEW (CMakeLists.txt, meson.build, configure.ac/configure.in, Makefile.am/Makefile.in — all via c_vs_cpp_tie_break)
VAL-008 NEW (shares manifests with C; disambiguated by c_vs_cpp_tie_break over .cpp/.cc/.cxx/.hpp/.hh/.hxx vs .c — .h NOT counted as it is ambiguous)
VAL-008 NEW (*.csproj extension, *.sln extension, global.json with sdk key)

per_language_status.unit_test: test_from_directory_detects_scala (+ scala_project_build_properties for the nested variant)

per_language_status.notes: Luau manifests are weak signals — many Luau projects ship without any. When no manifest is present the extension-majority fallback handles .luau files directly.

variants.Extension(&str): matches any file with a given extension (e.g. csproj for *.csproj)

special_cases.ConfigureAc: matches either configure.ac or configure.in (two-filename disjunction)
special_cases.MakefileAm: matches either Makefile.am or Makefile.in (two-filename disjunction)
special_cases.GlobalJson: only counts when the parsed JSON object contains top-level key `"sdk"` — avoids false positives on unrelated global.json files shipped by other tools

tie_break_design.h_header_policy: not counted (ambiguous between C and C++). A C++ project with .h headers is correctly identified as long as its .cpp-family count > 0.
tie_break_design.decision: Cpp if cpp_family > c_family, else C (default; simpler older language wins on ties and empty counts).
tie_break_design.walker_integration: uses crate::walker::walk_project, so node_modules/target/vendor etc. are transparently skipped.

tests_added.integration_test_pattern: tldr structure <tempdir> --format json --quiet — parse JSON, assert language field

verification.cargo_test_tldr_core_lib: 4675 passed / 0 failed / 340 ignored (VAL-002 baseline 4616 + VAL-007 20 + VAL-008 39 = 4675)
preexisting_failures_NOT_addressed: tldr-cli commands::bugbot::text_format::tests::test_text_format_generic_evidence_shows_numbers (pre-existing, noted in contract constraints as out-of-scope)
tldr-cli p2_multilang_tests (bounds/gvn subcommands removed pre-session — part of the 40-ish CLI tests referencing removed subcommands)

tie_break_rows_added: pre-existing: build.gradle.kts → Kotlin or Java (via gradle_kotlin_vs_java)

followups: Build.mill manifest (Scala's Mill build tool) was mentioned in the contract text but deferred — Mill project adoption is low enough that build.sbt + project/build.properties cover the vast majority of real-world Scala projects. Can be added in a follow-up if demand surfaces.

`.luau` files outside Roblox projects are rare enough that the extension-majority fallback remains the primary detection path for Luau projects without a manifest. The two manifests we did add (default.project.json and .luaurc) cover the two dominant Luau ecosystems (Rojo tooling, and luau-lsp/Luau LSP projects).
remaining_work_and_followups.known_good_parsers_baseline: All 18 tree-sitter grammars parse their minimal valid snippet with zero ERROR nodes as of this commit. The test_all_18_parsers_accept_minimal_valid_snippet unit test enforces this as a regression gate for future tree-sitter/ABI upgrades.

discoveries: The pre-VAL-008 detector used `m.file_name() -> &'static str` which assumed every manifest was a fixed filename. Adding pattern-matched manifests required a new ManifestMatcher enum and a matches_in(dir) method that dispatches by matcher kind.

global.json is ambiguous — it's shipped by .NET SDKs AND by unrelated tools (expo-cli, firebase-tools). Requiring the JSON to contain an `"sdk"` top-level key eliminates both false positives and flaky behaviour on broken JSON (which now returns false via the read-and-parse chain).
serde_json was already a tldr-core workspace dep, so the `"sdk"`-key validation path did not require new dependencies.

`.h` header ambiguity is a real-world issue (C/C++ projects frequently ship headers that outnumber source files). The tie-break policy of counting only .c vs .cpp-family (excluding .h entirely) correctly disambiguates projects such as `test_from_directory_cpp_with_h_headers_not_misdetected_as_c` (2×.cpp + 4×.h → Cpp, not C).
